quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Restriction enzyme"?

options.text: An endonuclease that cuts DNA at specific recognition sequences (4-8 bp palindromes), producing fragments with blunt or sticky ends
Ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase; the most abundant enzyme on Earth; fixes CO₂ but also has oxygenase activity (photorespiration)
Random orientation of homologous pairs at metaphase I, producing 2ⁿ possible gamete combinations (n = haploid number)
A DNA sequence upstream of a gene (e.g., TATA box) where RNA polymerase and transcription factors bind to initiate transcription

quiz_questions.explanation: "Restriction enzyme" is defined as: An endonuclease that cuts DNA at specific recognition sequences (4-8 bp palindromes), producing fragments with blunt or sticky ends. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Gel electrophoresis"?

options.text: Speciation driven by geographic isolation that prevents gene flow between populations, allowing independent evolution
A cluster of prokaryotic genes under control of a single promoter and operator, transcribed as one polycistronic mRNA (e.g., lac operon, trp operon)
A technique separating DNA, RNA, or protein fragments by size using an electric field; smaller fragments migrate faster through the agarose or polyacrylamide gel
A signaling molecule (hormone, neurotransmitter, growth factor) that binds specifically to a receptor protein

quiz_questions.explanation: "Gel electrophoresis" is defined as: A technique separating DNA, RNA, or protein fragments by size using an electric field; smaller fragments migrate faster through the agarose or polyacrylamide gel. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "PCR (polymerase chain reaction)"?

options.text: Benefits humans receive from ecosystems: provisioning (food, water), regulating (climate, flood control), cultural (recreation), and supporting (nutrient cycling, soil formation)
A cluster of prokaryotic genes under control of a single promoter and operator, transcribed as one polycistronic mRNA (e.g., lac operon, trp operon)
A technique that amplifies a specific DNA sequence exponentially using thermal cycling, primers, and Taq DNA polymerase; produces millions of copies in hours
The main replication enzyme in E. coli; synthesizes DNA 5'→3' by adding nucleotides complementary to the template strand

quiz_questions.explanation: "PCR (polymerase chain reaction)" is defined as: A technique that amplifies a specific DNA sequence exponentially using thermal cycling, primers, and Taq DNA polymerase; produces millions of copies in hours. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "CRISPR-Cas9"?

options.text: A gene-editing system using guide RNA to direct the Cas9 nuclease to cut DNA at a precise location, enabling precise gene knockout, correction, or insertion
The enzyme that synthesizes ATP as H⁺ flows down its concentration gradient from the intermembrane space back to the matrix
A phospholipid bilayer with embedded proteins that controls what enters and exits the cell; selectively permeable
RNA polymerase reads the DNA template strand 3'→5' and synthesizes complementary mRNA 5'→3'; occurs in the nucleus in eukaryotes

quiz_questions.explanation: "CRISPR-Cas9" is defined as: A gene-editing system using guide RNA to direct the Cas9 nuclease to cut DNA at a precise location, enabling precise gene knockout, correction, or insertion. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Recombinant DNA"?

options.text: No mutation, no migration, random mating, no selection, large population size; violations drive evolution
RuBisCO fixes O₂ instead of CO₂, producing a 2-carbon compound that wastes energy; more common at high temperatures
Germline mutations (in eggs/sperm) are passed to offspring; somatic mutations affect only the individual and are not inherited
DNA molecules formed by combining fragments from different sources, typically inserted into plasmid vectors and expressed in host organisms (bacteria, yeast)

quiz_questions.explanation: "Recombinant DNA" is defined as: DNA molecules formed by combining fragments from different sources, typically inserted into plasmid vectors and expressed in host organisms (bacteria, yeast). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "An endonuclease that cuts DNA at specific recognition sequences (4-8 bp palindromes), producing fragments with blunt or sticky ends"?

options.text: Phospholipid
Starch
Restriction enzyme
Species richness

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Restriction enzyme".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A technique separating DNA, RNA, or protein fragments by size using an electric field; smaller fragments migrate faster through the agarose or polyacrylamide gel"?

options.text: Photorespiration
Glycolysis
Gel electrophoresis
Allopatric speciation

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Gel electrophoresis".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A technique that amplifies a specific DNA sequence exponentially using thermal cycling, primers, and Taq DNA polymerase; produces millions of copies in hours"?

options.text: Energy coupling
Nucleus
Ecosystem services
PCR (polymerase chain reaction)

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "PCR (polymerase chain reaction)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A gene-editing system using guide RNA to direct the Cas9 nuclease to cut DNA at a precise location, enabling precise gene knockout, correction, or insertion"?

options.text: Cellular respiration (overall)
Electron transport chain
Genotype frequency
CRISPR-Cas9

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "CRISPR-Cas9".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "DNA molecules formed by combining fragments from different sources, typically inserted into plasmid vectors and expressed in host organisms (bacteria, yeast)"?

options.text: Ribosome
Recombination frequency
Adaptive radiation
Recombinant DNA

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Recombinant DNA".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Biotechnology Applications is accurate?

options.text: Minimum viable population (MVP), effective population size (Ne), and genetic rescue inform conservation management.
Translation is the process by which ribosomes synthesize proteins from mRNA templates.
Protein denaturation (loss of 3D shape from heat, pH, or chemical changes) destroys function, demonstrating that structure determines function—a central theme in AP Biology.
Biotechnology Applications encompasses the tools and techniques used to manipulate DNA.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Biotechnology Applications as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: DNA polymerase's proofreading (3'→5' exonuclease) and mismatch repair maintain a remarkably low error rate (~1 error per 10⁹ bases).
Sexual selection and artificial selection are special cases of selection that shape traits beyond survival.

Restriction enzymes cut DNA at specific sequences, gel electrophoresis separates fragments by size, PCR amplifies specific DNA regions, and recombinant DNA technology combines DNA from different sources.

The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium (p² + 2pq + q² = 1, p + q = 1) serves as a null hypothesis: populations are in equilibrium only when there is no mutation, migration, selection, drift, or non-random mating.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Biotechnology Applications as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: Genomic technologies (DNA sequencing, gene editing with CRISPR-Cas9, and bioinformatics) have revolutionized biology, medicine, agriculture, and forensics.
NADP⁺ is reduced to NADPH at PSI.
Population Ecology studies how populations grow, interact with their environment, and are regulated.
Proteins are polymers of amino acids linked by peptide bonds, with structure and function determined by the sequence (primary structure) and folding (secondary, tertiary, quaternary structures).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Biotechnology Applications as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: Biotechnology Applications encompasses the tools and techniques used to manipulate DNA.
Helicase unwinds the double helix, primase synthesizes RNA primers, and DNA polymerase III synthesizes new strands in the 5'→3' direction.
Protein denaturation (loss of 3D shape from heat, pH, or chemical changes) destroys function, demonstrating that structure determines function—a central theme in AP Biology.
Oxygen is the final electron acceptor, forming water.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Biotechnology Applications as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: Restriction enzymes cut DNA at specific sequences, gel electrophoresis separates fragments by size, PCR amplifies specific DNA regions, and recombinant DNA technology combines DNA from different sources.
Enzyme activity follows Michaelis-Menten kinetics characterized by Vmax (maximum velocity) and Km (substrate concentration at half Vmax).
Enzyme specificity arises from the precise 3D shape of the active site, which is determined by the amino acid sequence.
Adaptive radiation (rapid diversification from a single ancestor into multiple ecological niches) often follows mass extinctions or colonization of new environments.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Biotechnology Applications as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: Lead poisoning: lead (Pb²⁺) replaces zinc in enzymes, acting as a noncompetitive inhibitor and disrupting nervous system, kidney, and blood cell function
CRISPR-Cas9 clinical trials treating sickle cell disease by editing the BCL11A gene in patient stem cells to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production
Capillary action in plant xylem vessels transporting water against gravity from roots to leaves through cohesion-adhesion-tension
Active transport of ions (Na⁺/K⁺ ATPase) using ATP to pump 3 Na⁺ out and 2 K⁺ in per cycle, maintaining the resting membrane potential essential for nerve impulses

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Biotechnology Applications.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: Rising atmospheric CO₂ levels increasing carbon fixation rates in some plants (CO₂ fertilization effect) but potentially decreasing nutritional quality of crops
Amazon deforestation averaging ~10,000 km² per year, threatening the most biodiverse terrestrial ecosystem with an estimated 10% of all species on Earth
Firefly bioluminescence using ATP to power the oxidation of luciferin, converting chemical energy directly to light with remarkable efficiency
DNA fingerprinting using STR analysis for forensic identification, with Combined DNA Index System (CODIS) databases matching crime scene samples to individuals

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Biotechnology Applications.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Biotechnology Applications applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Cell membrane fluidity regulation through cholesterol insertion between phospholipids and adjustment of saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acid ratios
Golden Rice engineered with β-carotene biosynthesis genes to combat vitamin A deficiency in developing countries, preventing blindness in millions of children
Madagascar's unique biodiversity (90% of species found nowhere else) explained by 88 million years of isolation after separating from Africa and India
Ecosystem services valuation estimating that pollination by insects is worth $235-577 billion annually globally, providing economic justification for pollinator conservation

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Biotechnology Applications.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Biotechnology Applications?

options.text: Metabolic interconnections: Krebs cycle intermediates serve as precursors for amino acid synthesis, fatty acid synthesis, and gluconeogenesis
Prion diseases (mad cow, CJD) where misfolded proteins induce normal proteins to refold incorrectly, demonstrating the critical importance of protein conformation
CRISPR-Cas9 clinical trials treating sickle cell disease by editing the BCL11A gene in patient stem cells to reactivate fetal hemoglobin production
Coral bleaching when heat stress damages photosystem proteins in symbiotic zooxanthellae, disrupting photosynthesis and causing coral death

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Biotechnology Applications.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Biotechnology Applications in practice?

options.text: Caloric content of foods measured by the energy released during complete oxidation: carbohydrates ~4 kcal/g, lipids ~9 kcal/g, proteins ~4 kcal/g
Antibiotic targeting of prokaryotic-specific structures (70S ribosomes, cell wall synthesis) that differ from eukaryotic cells, enabling selective bacterial killing
DNA fingerprinting using STR analysis for forensic identification, with Combined DNA Index System (CODIS) databases matching crime scene samples to individuals
Cell membrane fluidity regulation through cholesterol insertion between phospholipids and adjustment of saturated vs. unsaturated fatty acid ratios

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Biotechnology Applications.
